connect.intro: This tool runs setup commands on your Android phone directly from your browser. Just plug in your phone via USB, pick an app, and choose what to do.
connect.title: Let's get started!
connect.subtitle: Plug your Android phone into this computer with a USB cable, then tap the button below.
connect.error: Something went wrong, but don't worry:
connect.authPrompt: Look at your phone now — you should see a popup asking for permission. Tap "Allow" to continue.
connect.resetHint: If connecting seems stuck, tap this to reset and try again.
selectApp.title: What app do you need help with?
selectApp.subtitle: Tap the app you want to set up.
selectCommand.subtitle: What would you like to do?
review.title: Ready to go?
review.multiStep: Multi-step command (reads current value, modifies, writes back)
review.riskSafe: This is safe and can be undone anytime.
review.riskModerate: This changes a system setting. You can reverse it later.
review.riskElevated: This is a bigger change — make sure this is what you want.
result.troubleTitle: Here are a few things to try:
result.troubleAppInstalled: Check that the app is installed on your phone
result.troubleDebugging: Make sure USB debugging is still turned on
result.troubleReplug: Try unplugging and plugging in your phone again
result.troubleFallback: If it still doesn't work, reach out for help below.
terminal.title: Activity Log
terminal.subtitle: Shows what's happening behind the scenes
browser.secureMessage: This page needs a secure connection. Please make sure the web address starts with https:// or open it on localhost.
browser.browserMessage: This tool needs Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge on a computer. Please switch to one of those browsers.
usbGuide.title: Need help setting up your phone?
usbGuide.step3: Tap Build number 7 times to unlock Developer Options
usbGuide.step6: Connect your phone to this computer with a USB cable
usbGuide.windowsMessage: You might need to install a USB driver for your phone.
usbGuide.linuxMessage: You may need to set up udev rules for your device.
usbGuide.tip: Tip: Make sure you're using a USB cable that can transfer data — some cables only charge.
error.claimInterface: Close Android Studio, other ADB clients, or browser tabs using this device, then retry.
error.timedOut: Make sure to tap "Allow" on the USB debugging prompt on your phone.
app.sdmaid-se.name: SD Maid SE
app.sdmaid-se.description: System cleaner for Android. Some features require ADB permissions to work around OS restrictions.
app.sdmaid-se.cmd.grant-write-secure-set: Grants the WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS permission so SD Maid can toggle accessibility services and modify system settings directly.
app.sdmaid-se.cmd.revoke-write-secure-se: Removes the WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS permission from SD Maid. Use this if you no longer need the extra features. Uninstalling the app also revokes all granted permissions.
